video:video.video:title: Trump says US military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang with help from Venezuela
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday that a “swift and lethal kinetic” U.S. strike has killed Héctor Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called “the infamous leader” of the Tren de Aragua gang.

video:video.video:title: Trump's name is gone from the Kennedy Center's facade, official says
video:video.video:description: Much of President Donald Trump's name was removed from the facade of Washington's Kennedy Center overnight after a federal judge issued an order.

video:video.video:title: Can you speak World Cup? We quizzed fans on soccer slang
video:video.video:description: From “squeaky bum time” to “parking the bus,” soccer has its own language. We asked World Cup fans if they could define some of the sport’s most iconic phrases.

video:video.video:title: What we know about a possible deal to end the Iran war
video:video.video:description: The apparent breakthrough in negotiations comes after Iran exchanged fire with the U.S. and Israel over three days this week, threatening to push the region into a full-scale war.

video:video.video:title: Delirious celebrations in New York as Knicks win first NBA championship in 53 years
video:video.video:description: The Knicks rallied from a 16-point deficit to beat the Spurs in San Antonio in Game 5 of the NBA Finals, turning pensive crowds euphoric at watch parties outside the team's Madison Square Garden arena and in parks and streets across the city.

video:video.video:title: Royal family attends Trooping of the Colour parade in London to mark King's official birthday
video:video.video:description: The parade was attended by Catherine, Princess of Wales along with her children, Prince George, Princess Charlotte and Prince Louis.

video:video.video:title: Pet owners in Gaza struggle to feed and treat their animals amid a shortage of veterinary supplies
video:video.video:description: Beyond a few facilities that treat mostly farm animals, just two veterinary clinics for common house pets remain operational across Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Paraguay fans upset by loss to USA in World Cup group opener
video:video.video:description: Paraguay fans in Asuncion reacted with dismay to their national team's defeat against co-host USA in the opening match of their group in the World Cup tournament.

video:video.video:title: UK's Trooping the Colour flypast captured in London skies
video:video.video:description: Aircraft from Britain's Royal Air Force were captured roaring through London skies on Saturday, as the King celebrated his official birthday with the Trooping of the Colour ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds march in Kathmandu for Nepal's Pride Month
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of members of Nepal's LGBTQ+ community and their supporters took to the streets of Kathmandu on Saturday to celebrate Pride Month.

video:video.video:title: Trump's name is gone from the Kennedy Center's façade
video:video.video:description: The removal of Trump's name closes one of the more unusual chapters in the history of the Kennedy Center, which began construction in 1964 and was dedicated to the memory of the slain president, Democrat John F.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse of workers covering signage at the Kennedy Center before removing Trump's name
video:video.video:description: Timelapse of workers covering signage at the Kennedy Center before removing Trump's name, after a judge ruled it was illegally added to the building.

video:video.video:title: Israeli warplanes strike Nabatiyeh in south Lebanon as troops advance in nearby villages
video:video.video:description: Heavy smoke was seen rising from an Israeli airstrike at the entrance of the city of Nabatiyeh. Israel's military appears to be trying to make as many gains as possible in case a U.S.

video:video.video:title: Collapsed tent during Virginia church's 20th anniversary celebration kills 1 and injures 22
video:video.video:description: A large tent collapsed during a Virginia church's 20th anniversary celebration on Friday evening, killing one person and injuring nearly two dozen others, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Trooping of the Colour ceremony in London on King Charles III's official birthday
video:video.video:description: Fast jets soared above London's Buckingham Palace on Saturday, leaving a trail of red, white and blue smoke, in a culmination of the king's official birthday celebrations.

video:video.video:title: Tens of thousands march through Rome in rival anti- and pro- migration rallies
video:video.video:description: The demonstrations came after a far-right citizens’ initiative seeking sweeping measures against migrants garnered enough support to be brought to Parliament.

video:video.video:title: Mayhem mars euphoria as New York City celebrates the Knicks' first championship in 53 years
video:video.video:description: Outside Madison Square Garden, a crowd watching on a big screen roared as the Knicks rallied from a 16-point deficit to beat the Spurs in San Antonio in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

video:video.video:title: ‘Spider-Man of Yemen’ dies in fall while climbing volcanic crater
video:video.video:description: Al-Qaqa Ibn Antar, 30, was climbing the steep walls of the Hardah Dam volcanic crater in the southern province of Dhale on Friday when he lost his grip and fell into the 120 metre (393 feet) crater, according to the Civil Defense Authority, which posted a short video capturing the moment of his fall

video:video.video:title: Brazil fans pack Copacabana Beach for World Cup opener against Morocco
video:video.video:description: Facing pressure to win its first World Cup title since 2002, five-time champion Brazil was outplayed early and needed Vinícius Júnior's 32nd-minute goal to gain a 1-1 draw against Morocco on Saturday in a pulsating, high-profile group match.

video:video.video:title: Tens of thousands take to the streets of Bucharest for pride parade
video:video.video:description: In ILGA-Europe’s 2025 Rainbow Map, which assesses the legal and policy landscape for LGBTQ+ people across Europe, Romania and Bulgaria ranked last among all 27 EU countries.

video:video.video:title: Mayor Mamdani celebrates as New York Knicks win NBA championship
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani celebrated the Knicks win at a bar in the West Village neighbourhood on Saturday night.

video:video.video:title: Britain detains sanctioned oil tanker believed to be linked to Russia’s shadow fleet
video:video.video:description: Armed British forces boarded and detained a sanctioned tanker Sunday that is suspected of being part of the Russian “shadow fleet,” shipping oil in violation of international sanctions over Moscow’s war on Ukraine, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Early results show Swiss voters reject right-wing's bid to cap population at 10 million
video:video.video:description: Voters in Switzerland cast their final ballots on Sunday on an initiative championed by the top right-wing party to cap the rich Alpine country’s population at 10 million.

video:video.video:title: Socceroos fans feeling positive after Australia wins 2-0 against Turkey in the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Many celebrated on Granville Street in downtown Vancouver where lines stretched from one bar to the next.

video:video.video:title: Twelve dead in plane crash during skydiving outing in Missouri, authorities say
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a pilot and 11 others were killed when a plane taking people on a skydiving outing crashed and caught fire in Missouri.

video:video.video:title: Plane crash in Missouri leaves 12 dead, authorities say
video:video.video:description: Authorities say 12 people were killed when a plane crashed in Missouri. The Missouri State Highway Patrol said in a statement that troopers were on the scene, assisting the Butler Police Department & Bates County Sheriff’s Office.

video:video.video:title: Scotland fans celebrate win over Haiti with bagpipes in Boston
video:video.video:description: Scotland fans, accompanied by bagpipers, marched to Fenway Park in Boston on Sunday ahead of "Scottish celebration night".

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather for the Netherlands' iconic Orange Parade
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Netherlands fans, and a few Japan fans gathered outside Dallas Stadium on Sunday to march in the iconic Orange Parade in advance of the teams' World Cup match.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military strikes Beirut suburbs
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military announced on Sunday it launched strikes on Beirut targeting Hezbollah infrastructure. Smoke could be seen rising over the Lebanese capital.

video:video.video:title: Helicopters collide over Rio de Janeiro, killing 6
video:video.video:description: Two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro on Sunday morning and crashed in the city’s western zone, killing all six people aboard, firefighters said.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 14
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, June 14, 2026: Israel's military said it launched strikes on Hezbollah targets in Beirut as President Donald Trump urged no further attacks; Authorities say 12 people were killed when a plane crashed in Missouri; The New York Knicks won their first NBA championship in 5

video:video.video:title: Tensions between Trump and European leaders ahead of G7 summit in France
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump has targeted the leaders of France, Germany, Italy and the UK with jabs and jibes of late.

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives at the Octagon to celebrate 80th birthday with UFC show on White House lawn
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump celebrated his 80th birthday Sunday with cage fighting on the White House lawn.

video:video.video:title: Protesters clash with police in Geneva ahead of G7 summit
video:video.video:description: Riot police fired tear gas and water cannons in clashes with stone-throwing youths during a protest on Sunday in Geneva against the G7 group of wealthy nations, a day before its leaders including U.S.

video:video.video:title: First responders on scene after Missouri plane crash kills 12
video:video.video:description: A plane carrying passengers planning to spend a sunny afternoon skydiving crashed Sunday in Missouri, killing all 12 people aboard, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Iran's deputy FM confirms deal with US to end the war, including in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran have reached an initial agreement to end their war and open the Strait of Hormuz, offering relief to the Gulf region and global economy more than three months since fighting began.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military strikes Beirut suburbs in the lead-up to anticipated US-Iran deal
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said it launched strikes on Beirut on Sunday targeting Hezbollah infrastructure, despite ongoing efforts to negotiate an end to the U.S.-Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Donald Trump celebrates 80th birthday with UFC show on White House lawn
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump turned 80 on Sunday and celebrated with a unique event: cage fighting on the White House lawn.

video:video.video:title: High-tech fishing sonar draws scrutiny as popularity with anglers booms
video:video.video:description: Forward-facing sonar allows users to see underwater in real time, including their lures and fish. But concerns about the technology’s potential impact have prompted state officials to take a closer look.

video:video.video:title: Russian attack sets fire to centuries-old religious site in Kyiv and kills 5 in Kharkiv
video:video.video:description: A large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine killed five rescuers in Kharkiv and wounded at least 20 people in the capital Kyiv on Monday as strikes set apartment buildings ablaze and sparked a fire at one of the country’s most significant religious landmarks.

video:video.video:title: Orphaned children in Gaza put a huge strain on those left to care for them
video:video.video:description: As the death toll in Gaza surpassed 73,000 on Sunday, the overwhelming number of orphaned children who have lost one or both parents have put a huge strain on those left to care for them.

video:video.video:title: Navy Blue Angels and Air Force Thunderbirds hold joint flyover before White House UFC event
video:video.video:description: The Navy’s Blue Angels and Air Force Thunderbirds zipped overhead on Sunday as part of a flyover to celebrate U.S.

video:video.video:title: Deal is reached to end Iran war, as Trump orders stop to US naval blockade
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran have reached an agreement to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. This offers relief to the global economy more than three months since fighting began.

video:video.video:title: Tentative deal on ending the Iran war sends stocks soaring while oil prices fall
video:video.video:description: Stocks rallied in Asia on Monday after a tentative deal was announced on ending the Iran war and reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Fans react to Curaçao's 7-1 loss against Germany in FIFA World Cup debut
video:video.video:description: Curaçao became the smallest country ever to compete at the World Cup on Sunday when it faced four-time champion Germany on in Houston.
